subject: Computer Networking
title: Mobile Network Privacy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mobile Network Privacy.

hook.text: Your phone reveals your location to cell towers constantly. That data tells a detailed story about your life.

buildup.text: Carriers know which towers you connect to and when. That creates a precise log of your movements.

discovery.text: IMSI catchers — fake cell towers — trick your phone into connecting. Law enforcement and hackers both use them.

twist.text: 5G improved subscriber privacy with SUPI encryption. Your identity is hidden from eavesdroppers on the radio link.

climax.text: VPNs protect your data but not your location. Only airplane mode truly stops your phone from broadcasting its position.

punchline.text: Your phone is a tracking device you carry willingly.

quiz.question: What is an IMSI catcher?
options: A fake cell tower that tricks phones into connecting
A tool that measures internet speed
A device that blocks all wireless signals
